007 2006 2005 2004 2003 2002 2001 2000 1999 1998 1997 1996 1995 1994 1993 1992 1991 1990 1989 1988 1987 1986 1985 1984 Skip to sidebar A Code Generation Framework for Actor-Oriented Models with Partial Evaluation Gang Zhou, Man-Kit Leung, and Edward

l Evaluation Gang Zhou, Man-Kit Leung, and Edward A. Lee in Proceedings of International Conference on Embedded Software and Systems 2007 , LNCS 4523, pp. 786-799, Daegu, South Korea, May 14-16, 2007, Y.-H. Lee et al. (Eds.) © Springer-Verlag Berlin

Y.-H. Lee et al. (Eds.) © Springer-Verlag Berlin Heidelberg 2007 Prepublished version Published version (see also the earlier Technical Report .) ABSTRACT Embedded software requires concurrency formalisms other than threads and mutexes used in tradi

lisms other than threads and mutexes used in traditional programming languages like C. Actor-oriented design presents a high level abstraction for composing concurrent components. However, high level abstraction often introduces overhead and results

abstraction often introduces overhead and results in slower system. We address the problem of generating efficient implementation for the systems with such a high level description. We use partial evaluation as an optimized compilation technique for

luation as an optimized compilation technique for actor-oriented models. We use a helper-based mechanism, which results in flexible and extensible code generation framework. The end result is that the benefit offered by high level abstraction comes w

benefit offered by high level abstraction comes with (almost) no performance penalty. The code generation framework has been released in open source form as part of Ptolemy II 6.0.1. Contact Update Help Update Copyright © 1999 - UC Regents; all righ
